sentence: As nuves cobren o ceo e a terra fria.
ipa: as̺ ˈnuves̺ ˈkobɾen o ˈt͡seo e a ˈtera ˈfɾia
gloss_en: The clouds cover the sky and the cold earth.
notes: Affricate-free close-mid vocalism [t͡seo] for <ceo> and trilled <rr> [tera] per maia1986; unreduced tonic mids per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O pastor guarda seus gãados nos montes.
ipa: o paˈs̺toɾ ˈɡwaɾda ˈs̺ews̺ ˈɡãados̺ nos̺ ˈmontes̺
gloss_en: The shepherd guards his herds in the hills.
notes: Archaic plural <gãados>/<montes> and unreduced final vowels per maia1986; sandhi at word junctions per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A mia senhor mora preto do rio.
ipa: a ˈmia s̺eˈɲoɾ ˈmoɾa ˈpɾeto do ˈɾio
gloss_en: My lady dwells near the river.
notes: Cantiga possessive <mia> and palatal <nh> [ɲ] per freixeiro_mato1998; close-mid [moɾa] per maia1986.

sentence: Amigo, id agora pola carreira do val.
ipa: aˈmiɡo ˈid aˈɡoɾa ˈpola kaˈrejɾa do ˈval
gloss_en: Friend, go now along the road of the valley.
notes: Second-person plural imperative <id> and contraction <pola> per freixeiro_mato1998; trilled <rr> in <carreira> and coda /l/ in <val> per maia1986.

sentence: O pescado do mar era saboroso e fresco.
ipa: o peˈs̺kado do ˈmaɾ ˈeɾa s̺aboˈɾoz̺o e ˈfɾes̺ko
gloss_en: The fish from the sea was tasty and fresh.
notes: Unreduced pretonic and close mids per mateus_dandrade2000; <e fresco> vowel liaison per maia1986.

sentence: U mora vossa madre nesta vila?
ipa: ˈu ˈmoɾa ˈvos̺a ˈmadɾe ˈnes̺ta ˈvila
gloss_en: Where does your mother live in this town?
notes: Archaic locative interrogative <u> and possessive <vossa> per freixeiro_mato1998; unreduced vocalism per maia1986.

sentence: Quantos dias durou a vossa viagem?
ipa: kwaˈntos̺ ˈdias̺ duˈɾow a ˈvos̺a viˈad͡ʒem
gloss_en: How many days did your journey last?
notes: Retained <ou> diphthong in <durou> [duɾow] per maia1986; <durou a> liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Se eu ar podesse, iria veer mia senhor.
ipa: s̺e ˈew ˈaɾ poˈdes̺e iˈɾia veˈeɾ ˈmia s̺eˈɲoɾ
gloss_en: If I could yet, I would go to see my lady.
notes: Cantiga adverb <ar> and hiatus infinitive <veer> [veeɾ] per freixeiro_mato1998; conditional register per maia1986.

sentence: Comprei pan e vinho ena feira.
ipa: koˈmpɾej ˈpan e ˈviɲo ˈena ˈfejɾa
gloss_en: I bought bread and wine at the fair.
notes: Apocopated <pan> and contraction <ena> (en+a) with palatal <nh> per freixeiro_mato1998; retained <ei> in <comprei>/<feira> per maia1986.

sentence: Cras irei pelo caminho da montanha.
ipa: ˈkɾas̺ iˈɾej pelo kaˈmiɲo da moˈntaɲa
gloss_en: Tomorrow I will go along the mountain path.
notes: Archaic adverb <cras> (tomorrow) per freixeiro_mato1998; palatal <nh> [ɲ] and retained <ei> per maia1986.

sentence: Chamade o moço que traga o cavalo.
ipa: t͡ʃaˈmade o ˈmot͡so ke ˈtɾaɡa o kaˈvalo
gloss_en: Call the boy to bring the horse.
notes: Second-plural imperative <chamade> per freixeiro_mato1998; affricate <ç> [t͡s] in <moço> [mot͡so] per maia1986.

sentence: Boa manhã, amigo; como vos vai a saude?
ipa: ˈboa maˈɲã aˈmiɡo ˈkomo vos̺ ˈvaj a ˈs̺awde
gloss_en: Good morning, friend; how does your health fare?
notes: Clitic <vos> address and <vai a> liaison per freixeiro_mato1998; palatal <nh> and close mids per maia1986.

sentence: Mais val bon amigo ca parente.
ipa: ˈmajs̺ ˈval ˈbon aˈmiɡo ˈka paˈɾente
gloss_en: A good friend is worth more than a kinsman.
notes: Proverbial <ca> comparative and apocopated <bon>/<val> per freixeiro_mato1998; coda /l/ in <val> per maia1986.

sentence: O gato negro dorme sobo o banco.
ipa: o ˈɡato ˈneɡɾo ˈdoɾme ˈs̺obo o ˈbanko
gloss_en: The black cat sleeps under the bench.
notes: Archaic preposition <sobo> (under) and close-mid vocalism per freixeiro_mato1998; <sobo o> liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Aquele rato fugiu pera o buraco.
ipa: aˈkele ˈɾato fuˈd͡ʒiu ˈpeɾa o buˈɾako
gloss_en: That mouse fled into the hole.
notes: Archaic directional <pera> and <fugiu o> junction per freixeiro_mato1998; close-mid vocalism per maia1986.

sentence: O mundo enteiro fala deste feito.
ipa: o ˈmundo eˈntejɾo ˈfala ˈdes̺te ˈfejto
gloss_en: The whole world speaks of this deed.
notes: Archaic <enteiro>/<feito> with retained <ei> per maia1986; unreduced tonic mids per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol luz sobre las colinas verdes.
ipa: o ˈs̺ol ˈluz̺ ˈs̺obɾe las̺ koˈlinas̺ ˈveɾdes̺
gloss_en: The sun shines over the green hills.
notes: Archaic article <las> and coda /l/ in <sol> per freixeiro_mato1998; close mids per maia1986.

sentence: A serra e alta e o rio corre forte.
ipa: a ˈs̺era e ˈalta e o ˈɾio ˈkore ˈfoɾte
gloss_en: The mountain range is high and the river runs strong.
notes: Trilled <rr> in <serra>/<corre> per maia1986; <serra e>/<e alta> liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Minha filha colheu as flores no vale.
ipa: ˈmiɲa ˈfiʎa koˈʎew as̺ ˈfloɾes̺ no ˈvale
gloss_en: My daughter gathered the flowers in the valley.
notes: Palatals <nh>/<lh> [ɲ ʎ] in <minha>/<filha> per maia1986; retained hiatus <colheu> per freixeiro_mato1998.

sentence: A cidade ten muitas ruas e pontes.
ipa: a t͡siˈdade ˈten ˈmujtas̺ ˈɾuas̺ e ˈpontes̺
gloss_en: The city has many streets and bridges.
notes: Affricate <c> before front vowel [t͡sidade] and archaic verb <ten> per maia1986; <ruas e> liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.
